violations.text: The presence of mice is not controlled as evidenced by droppings throughout kitchen and basement. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment is to clean any and all droppings using appropriate means of sanitization to monitor for new activity. Establishment is to continue to work with pest control, and is to increase pest control as frequently as needed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/18/2025

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. PIC has installed a new door sweep, but a couple opening remain. Establishment is to make door completely tight fitting. New Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2025

The chest freezer lid was observed broken and in poor repair. The walk-in cooler handle was observed broken in poor repair. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/23/2025

The floors throughout the kitchen have an accumulation of soil residue, droppings, and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/18/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishments RICFSM has expired. Establishment is to renew/re-apply with RIDOH. New Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2025

Dishes and utensils were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Dishes were removed from sink at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/09/2025

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Hand soap was added at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/09/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils can opener were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Can opener was moved to ware washing area at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/09/2025

Various food was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 12/09/2025

ROP salmon and tilapia was observed thawing with ROP packaging still intact. A hole was observed in package. Salmon and tilapia were taken completely out of package at the time of inspection. Proper thawing methods reviewed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/09/2025

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 12/12/2025

The presence of mice is not controlled as evidenced by -An accumulation of droppings in basement -An accumulation of droppings throughout the kitchen -2 dead mice in basement -Dead rat by dumpster The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment is to clean any and all droppings/dead mice using appropriate means of sanitization. Establishment is to continue to work with pest control and is to have a pest control report for inspector at the time of next inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 12/09/2025
Various foods were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/09/2025

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/09/2025

The chest freezer lid was observed broken and in poor repair. The walk-in cooler handle was observed broken in poor repair. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/23/2025

The cutting boards in the storage area are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 12/09/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the refrigeration units and shelves have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 12/09/2025
The outside refuse area was observed with an accumulation of debris and a dead rat. Refuse areas must be maintained. New Violation. Correct By: 12/09/2025

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue, droppings, and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 12/09/2025

At the time of inspection, there was not adequate lighting in the kitchen area. Lights were observed not working. Service arrived to fix lighting issue at the end of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/09/2025

Measuring cup was observed in the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Creamer was observed in the hand-washing sink in the service area. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2024

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arrranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2024

Observed dead cockroaches on glue boards in basement. Dead or trapped birds, insects, rodents, and other pests shall be removed from the premises at a frequency that presents their accumulation, decomposition or the attraction of pests. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings in kitchen. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2024
Walk-in cooler located in the kitchen area was not in good repair, observed active leak. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the hand-washing sink ledge have an accumulation of rodent droppings. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2024

The floors in the ware-washing area has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2024

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arrranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (prep unit) were observed with an accumulation of dead flies. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2024

The presence of roaches is not controlled as evidenced by live roaches in basement (dirty linens) and back kitchen. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2024
Walk-in cooler was observed with an active leak. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2024
The floors in the kitchen have an accumulation of dead roaches. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2024
No person in charge was present as needed at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024
Measuring cup was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by cart. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

Raw animal foods (eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (milk) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (storage containers, prep tables) were observed with an accumulation of roaches. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

The presence of: - Roaches is not controlled as evidenced by live and dead roaches in the establishment. - Insects is not controlled as evidenced by an abundance of small flying insects on food storage. - Rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings throughout establishment. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024
Walk-in cooler was not in good repair, observed active leak. No foods were stored under leak at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of shelving throughout kitchen have an accumulation of rodent droppings. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024
The walk-in cooler has an accumulation of rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024
The ledge in the back area (stairs) is in poor repair, observed incomplete seal with exposed wood. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024
The non-food contact surfaces under the flattop grills and cooking stations have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 04/03/2024
Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2024

The kitchen does not have a hand-washing sink. Hand-washing sinks must be located to allow convenient use by employees in food preparation, food dispensing, and ware-washing areas. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2024
Cabbage was observed with gnawing, rodent droppings, and rodent hair. PIC voluntarily disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2024

Raw animal foods (raw whole shell eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as milk) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2024

Food contact surfaces of prep table in walk-in was observed with an accumulation of rodent droppings. The food contact surface of the ice machine was observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2024

Prepared foods (beans, macaroni salad, etc.) in the walk-in were observed held at 49-53 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2024

Various food items in the walk-in were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2024

The presence of rodents and roaches is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings throughout the perimeters of the facility, in the walk-in, as well as cockroach carcasses. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2024
Inspector observed a hole in wall at the juncture of the floor and wall in basement. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2024

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2024

The ice scoop was observed stored in the ice with handle in ice between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Staff member took ice scoop to be cleaned and sanitized and replaced with a new scoop placed on a clean and sanitized surface. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2024
The walk-in cooler was not in good repair. Inspector observed ambient temperature measuring 52-54 degrees Fahrenheit. PIC stated that service was scheduled. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the walk-in and food prep units have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2024
The area surrounding the refuse receptacles have an accumulation of litter and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2024

The floors, walls, ledges, baseboards throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue, food debris, and rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2024
